commonIssues:
Deaths not registered within 21 days face penalties and require magistrate approval, with fees ranging from ₹100–₹500 depending on delay duration.

How to fix:

  • 21–30 days: Apply at the local Urban Local Body (ULB) or Gram Panchayat with a late fee of ₹100.
  • 30 days to 1 year: Apply with an affidavit and late fee at the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office.
  • After 1 year: Obtain an Executive Magistrate order, then apply at the local body.
  • Apply online via e-Nagarsewa UP Portal.
  • Helpline: 1800-180-5555 (UP e-District Helpline)

Difficulties using the e-Nagarsewa portal include technical errors in submitting documents, OTP failures, and server downtime.

How to fix:

  • Use e-Nagarsewa UP on a desktop browser (Chrome/Firefox) in non-peak hours.
  • Clear browser cache and cookies. Try incognito mode if OTP is not arriving.
  • If portal is down, submit the application offline at the local Nagar Palika/Nagar Nigam office.
  • For technical support, call: 1800-180-5555 or email: dda-up@nic.in.

Incorrect data (age, date, name) in the application leads to rejection and requires time-consuming correction procedures.

How to fix:

  • Cross-verify all details against the hospital death record, Aadhaar, and other ID proofs before submission.
  • If rejected, prepare a Notarised Affidavit explaining the correct information, with supporting documents.
  • Submit the affidavit to the local Registrar's (Nagar Panchayat/Nagar Palika) office for re-processing.
  • Track your application at e-Nagarsewa UP.

Missing crucial documents like medical records, proof of birth, or affidavits, combined with slow processes in local bodies and Gram Panchayats, cause significant delays.

How to fix:

  • For hospital deaths: Request the MCCD (Medical Certificate of Cause of Death) from the hospital before discharge.
  • For home deaths: Get a certificate from a Registered Medical Practitioner (RMP) and a Panchanama from police.
  • For Gram Panchayat delays, escalate to the Block Development Officer (BDO) in writing.
  • Raise grievance via UP Jansunwai Portal or call 1076.
